winner: Candidate 1 â€” Faithful asyncio port (one query() per graph node, plain-Python driver)

scores.candidate: Candidate 1: Faithful asyncio port (one query() per node, explicit Python driver)
scores.rationale: (a) subscription-auth 9/10: pops ANTHROPIC_API_KEY at client construction, asserts CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, setting_sources=[] isolation, Credential Manager storage â€” metered billing is made impossible, not merely deprioritized. (b) unattended reliability 9/10: zero model-orchestration surface (Python owns all control flow), retry wrapper, per-ticker isolation, checkpoint resume, trading-day skip, file logging + exit codes; weakest spot is opt-in (not always-on) checkpointing and no single-instance lock. (c) fidelity 9/10: highest of the three â€” verbatim prompts, exact count>=2N/3N semantics checked after each speaker, exact tier assignment, invoke_structured_or_freetext parity, byte-compatible memory log; crucially it is the ONLY candidate that correctly vendors memory/rating/schemas (verified: tradingagents/agents/__init__.py eagerly imports all LangChain factories, so parent imports of those modules are LangChain-coupled). (d) maintenance 8/10: simple driver, single LLM seam, thin parent-import surface (dataflows only, verified framework-free); vendored prompt/schema/memory copies are a manual-diff obligation. (e) testability 9/10: MockAgentClient at the one seam, socket guard, dry-run, gated live smoke â€” default pytest cannot construct the SDK client at all.

scores.candidate: Candidate 2: Single-session orchestrator with SDK subagents + pipeline MCP state tools
scores.rationale: (a) 8/10: same token scrub/assert pattern, sound. (b) 6/10: months of unattended runs hinge on an LLM orchestrator invoking 12 subagents in the right order; the design itself admits drift, self-correction token burn, and uncertainty about whether subagents inherit in-process MCP servers in the pinned SDK version â€” too many evolving SDK surfaces (AgentDefinition, hooks, Task fan-out) for a set-and-forget scheduler job. (c) 7/10: contracts are protected by tool-enforced state machines (clever), but the execution path (orchestrator stage prompts, prompt-encouraged save tools instead of forced structured output) diverges materially from the original deterministic graph, and it imports schemas/memory/rating through the LangChain-coupled agents package. (d) 5/10: most code, most concepts (2 MCP servers, hook tables, corrective-retry prompts, 12 AgentDefinitions, orchestrator prompt contract). (e) 7/10: MockBackend driving the real pipeline-tool handlers is genuinely good, but the riskiest layer â€” real model orchestration â€” is exactly the layer mock mode cannot exercise. Best graftable ideas: nested-.git/.python-version packaging fix (verified real), message_tool.log tee, Proactor-loop and PYTHONUTF8 Windows notes, per-stage timings in decisions.jsonl, insider-transactions binding flag.

scores.candidate: Candidate 3: Sequential driver reusing parent agents modules via import
scores.rationale: (a) 8/10: same fail-fast + key-pop; User-scope env var for the token is simpler but plaintext-in-registry (Credential Manager is better as primary). (b) 8/10: adds what C1 missed â€” single-instance lock file with stale detection, always-on cheap JSON checkpointing, MultipleInstances IgnoreNew, ExecutionTimeLimit 2h, direct venv-python invocation. (c) 8/10: same verbatim-prompt and count-semantics discipline as C1, but imports schemas/render_*/parse_rating/memory from tradingagents.agents â€” verified to execute the LangChain-importing package __init__, importing langchain_core et al. at runtime; works today inside the workspace venv but couples the port to parent refactors and contradicts the clean-port goal. (d) 7/10: lean driver, but the agents-package import coupling is the single biggest maintenance landmine across all three designs (acknowledged in its own risk list with vendoring as fallback â€” should have been the default). (e) 9/10: best test hygiene â€” conftest deletes both auth env vars so an accidental real-SDK path fails loudly, fixture-based route_to_vendor loader, parent-seam import canary, register_task -DryRun, distinct --dry-run vs --mock semantics.

final_design.title: tradingagents-cc: Faithful Claude Agent SDK port of TradingAgents â€” plain asyncio driver, one query() per graph node, subscription-auth only, keyless yfinance data, always-on JSON checkpointing, Windows Task Scheduler routine (Candidate 1 architecture + reliability/test grafts from Candidates 2 and 3)
final_design.architecture:
DESIGN STANCE (Candidate 1, confirmed by audit): the LangGraph graph is deterministic sequential control flow with three bounded loops (per-analyst tool loop, 2N bull/bear turns, 3N risk turns), so LangGraph is replaced by an explicit asyncio driver in pipeline.py that makes ONE claude_agent_sdk.query() per graph node and threads a plain state dict (AgentState minus the messages channel) between stages. Python owns 100% of control flow; the model is never trusted with orchestration (this is the decisive reliability difference vs Candidate 2). Package layout is FLAT (single tradingagents_cc package, no subpackages) so every file in the plan is independently implementable.

PRE-IMPLEMENTATION REPO HYGIENE (verified necessary): delete the nested C:\Users\randl\Documents\GitHub\TradingAgents\tradingagents-cc\.git directory (uv init artifact â€” otherwise the parent repo records a gitlink and the port's files are never tracked) and re-pin tradingagents-cc\.python-version from 3.14 to 3.13 to match the workspace root (avoids uv resolving a second interpreter and 3.14 wheel gaps). The root pyproject already lists tradingagents-cc as a workspace member.

LLM SEAM (client.py): AgentClient protocol with async run(role, prompt, *, system_prompt, model, tools_server, allowed_tools, output_schema, max_turns) -> AgentResult{text, structured, usage{llm_calls,tool_calls,tokens_in,tokens_out}, num_turns, tool_call_log}. SdkAgentClient wraps query(prompt=..., options=ClaudeAgentOptions(...)). Auth guarantee at construction: os.environ.pop('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', None) with a logged warning, then assert CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is present (or allow interactive Claude Code login for dev); fail fast with remediation text ('re-run claude setup-token') and a distinct AuthError. Every options object sets setting_sources=[] (isolate from ~/.claude CLAUDE.md/skills/MCP), permission_mode='acceptEdits', explicit disallowed_tools covering all built-ins (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch, Task, NotebookEdit), plus a can_use_tool deny-by-default callback as second enforcement. Model tiers preserved exactly: quick_think_llm (default 'claude-sonnet-4-6') for the 4 analysts, bull/bear, trader, 3 risk debators, Reflector; deep_think_llm (default 'claude-opus-4-8') ONLY for Research Manager and Portfolio Manager; documented budget override quick='claude-haiku-4-5'; optional effort passthrough for the deep tier. Pin claude-agent-sdk in pyproject and verify output_format/structured_output field names against the installed version during Wave 0 (Candidate 2's SDK-drift caution). Windows note baked into client.py docstring and README: the SDK's subprocess transport requires the default Proactor event loop â€” never install a Selector policy.

NODE-BY-NODE MAPPING (exact original semantics, from the audit):
1. Phase-B reflection (reflection.py, mirrors _resolve_pending_entries): per same-ticker pending memory entry, fetch_returns via yfinance (ticker + SPY closes, holding_days=5, +7-day weekend buffer, alpha = raw - spy); one quick-tier no-tool max_turns=1 query() with the verbatim Reflector prompts; memory_log.batch_update_with_outcomes atomic rewrite; (None,None,None) on data failure leaves the entry pending. Skipped entirely in mock mode.
2. Initial state (state.py): create_initial_state(ticker, trade_date, past_context) zeroing InvestDebateState/RiskDebateState exactly per the original TypedDicts; safe_ticker_component (imported from tradingagents.dataflows.utils â€” framework-free, verified) validates the ticker before any path use.
3. Analysts (selected_analysts order, sequential): one query() each. system_prompt = verbatim collaboration wrapper + per-analyst system_message (fundamentals 1-tuple bug FIXED) + current_date + build_instrument_context + language instruction; mcp_servers={'data': in-process SDK MCP server}; allowed_tools = exact per-analyst subset (market: mcp__data__get_stock_data + get_indicators; social: get_news; news: get_news + get_global_news; fundamentals: the 4 statement tools); config flag bind_insider_to_news=False restores the original ToolNode's get_insider_transactions binding when enabled. The SDK agentic loop IS the ReAct loop (replaces should_continue_* + ToolNode); a fresh query() per analyst IS create_msg_delete. max_turns=config['max_analyst_turns'] (default 12) replaces the dead recursion_limit=100. ResultMessage.result -> market_report/sentiment_report/news_report/fundamentals_report. While iterating the message stream the client tees every tool_use block to message_tool.log ('HH:MM:SS [Tool Call] name(args)') â€” Candidate 2 graft reproducing the original artifact.
4. Bull/Bear debate (conditional_logic.py ported verbatim): Bull first (fixed edge); loop with count checked AFTER each speaker: count >= 2*max_debate_rounds -> done; else Bear if current_response.startswith('Bull') else Bull. Each turn = no-tool quick query() max_turns=1 on the verbatim f-string prompt (4 reports + history + opponent current_response); Python applies the exact 'Bull Analyst: '/'Bear Analyst: ' prefixes, history/bull_history/bear_history appends, count += 1. Depth presets 1/3/5 set BOTH round knobs.
5. Research Manager (deep, structured): output_format={'type':'json_schema','schema': ResearchPlan.model_json_schema()} (vendored Pydantic, shallow, enum recommendation). Success: model_validate(structured_output) -> render_research_plan -> investment_plan + judge_decision/current_response. On subtype error_max_structured_output_retries or validation failure: ONE plain free-text query() with the same prompt â€” exact invoke_structured_or_freetext semantics; fallback occurrences flagged in decisions.jsonl.
6. Trader (quick, structured TraderProposal, same fallback): rendered markdown ALWAYS ends 'FINAL TRANSACTION PROPOSAL: **{ACTION}**' -> trader_investment_plan, sender='Trader'.
7. Risk rotation: Aggressive first (fixed edge); verbatim should_continue_risk_analysis: count >= 3*max_risk_discuss_rounds -> PM; latest_speaker startswith 'Aggressive' -> Conservative; 'Conservative' -> Neutral; else Aggressive. Each debator: quick, max_turns=1, no tools, verbatim prompt; sets latest_speaker exactly 'Aggressive'/'Conservative'/'Neutral', current_<self>_response = '{Role} Analyst: {content}', history append, count += 1.
8. Portfolio Manager (deep, structured PortfolioDecision, same fallback): verbatim prompt with 5-tier Rating Scale, investment_plan, trader_investment_plan, the 'Lessons from prior decisions and outcomes:' line ONLY when past_context non-empty (issue-#572 guard), risk history, language instruction. render_pm_decision guarantees '**Rating**: X' -> final_trade_decision, judge_decision, latest_speaker='Judge'.
9. Signal: vendored parse_rating â€” pure two-pass regex over Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell, default 'Hold', zero LLM, byte-for-byte.
10. Persistence: reporting.py tees section markdown live after each stage (postmortem value for failed unattended runs), then writes complete_report.md (I..V layout), full_states_log_{date}.json (same keys as _log_state), appends one decisions.jsonl line, then memory_log.store_decision (Phase A pending entry â€” fixing the original CLI's bypass of the memory path), clears the checkpoint.

CHECKPOINTING (Candidate 3 graft: ALWAYS-ON, config-disablable â€” cheap JSON, no reason to make unattended resume opt-in): after every completed node, atomic temp + os.replace write of {thread_id, completed: [exact original node-name strings], state} to {data_cache_dir}/cc_checkpoints/{TICKER}/{thread_id}.json with thread_id = sha256(f'{TICKER}:{date}')[:16] (parent-identical derivation). Re-running same ticker+date resumes after the last completed node (mid-debate resumes naturally from persisted counts); new date starts fresh; cleared on success; CLI clear-checkpoints and resume subcommands.

ERROR HANDLING: (1) every query() goes through a retry wrapper â€” 3 attempts, exponential backoff 5s/15s/45s + jitter on CLIConnectionError/process errors/429-shaped failures (SDK has no built-in scheduled-job backoff). (2) every MCP tool handler wraps its body in try/except and returns {'content':[{'type':'text','text':f'Error: {e}'}], 'is_error': True} â€” mirrors original error-string tools and avoids the documented pitfall that an uncaught handler exception kills the whole query(). (3) a node failing after retries raises StageError: run exits nonzero, checkpoint preserves progress, next scheduled run resumes. (4) routine catches per-ticker failures, continues remaining tickers, aggregates exit code (0 ok/skip, 1 partial/stage failure, 2 auth/fatal). (5) stats contract preserved: AssistantMessage count -> llm_calls, tool_use blocks -> tool_calls, ResultMessage usage -> tokens_in/out, plus per-node stage timings (Candidate 2 graft) into decisions.jsonl.

CONCURRENCY: strictly sequential â€” nodes within a run (data dependencies) and tickers within the routine (protects the single memory-log file's atomic-replace writes, throttles yfinance). asyncio because the SDK is async-first, not for parallelism; one asyncio.run() per invocation; Proactor loop untouched.
final_design.data_strategy: Reuse the parent tradingagents.dataflows package directly via the uv workspace â€” VERIFIED safe in this judging pass: tradingagents/__init__.py and tradingagents/dataflows/__init__.py are both 0 bytes, so importing dataflows executes zero LangChain code, and the audit confirms the modules import only pandas/yfinance/stockstats/requests/dateutil. tradingagents-cc/pyproject.toml declares dependencies = ['tradingagents', 'claude-agent-sdk' (pinned), 'pydantic>=2', 'yfinance'] with [tool.uv.sources] tradingagents = { workspace = true }. This buys byte-identical tool output strings, the daily OHLCV CSV cache, yf_retry 2s/4s/8s 429 backoff, look-ahead-bias guards, and safe_ticker_component, with automatic pickup of parent fixes. The import surface is deliberately thin and canary-tested (Candidate 3 graft): interface.route_to_vendor, dataflows.config.set_config, dataflows.utils.safe_ticker_component only. NOTHING is imported from tradingagents.agents.* â€” its package __init__ eagerly imports every LangChain factory (verified, 1400-byte __init__ importing all create_* functions), which is why memory/rating/schemas/prompts are vendored instead. Zero paid keys by default: tools_data.py defines the 9 tools with the exact original names/signatures (get_stock_data(symbol,start_date,end_date); get_indicators(symbol,indicator,curr_date,look_back_days=30) preserving comma-split + per-indicator ValueError collection; get_fundamentals; get_balance_sheet/get_cashflow/get_income_statement(ticker,freq='quarterly',curr_date=None); get_news(ticker,start_date,end_date); get_global_news(curr_date,look_back_days=7,limit=5); get_insider_transactions(ticker)) as claude_agent_sdk @tool handlers on one in-process create_sdk_mcp_server(name='data'), each calling route_to_vendor via asyncio.to_thread (bodies are sync/blocking; must not block the in-process MCP event loop), with ToolAnnotations(readOnlyHint=True) so Claude batches parallel-safe fetches within a turn. Before each run, dataflows.config.set_config() propagates our config so data_vendors/tool_vendors flow into the parent module-global â€” defaults all 'yfinance' (OHLCV via yf.download + daily CSV cache, 13 indicators computed locally by stockstats, fundamentals/statements/insider via yf.Ticker, news via Ticker.get_news + yf.Search). Alpha Vantage remains exactly as upstream: optional, reached only if the user sets a vendor to 'alpha_vantage' AND exports ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY; never required, never read by cc code. Phase-B outcome returns (ticker + SPY) are also keyless yfinance. Per-analyst tool subsets pinned in ANALYST_TOOLSETS and enforced twice (allowed_tools + can_use_tool deny-by-default). The only network dependencies of a default run are Yahoo Finance and the Claude subscription endpoint.
final_design.memory_strategy: Carry forward the parent's v0.2.4 design â€” it is already keyless; FinancialSituationMemory (OpenAI embeddings + ChromaDB v1, BM25 v2) was deliberately deleted upstream and is NOT reintroduced. The live mechanism is TradingMemoryLog: a pure-stdlib append-only markdown decision log with zero API calls. tradingagents-cc VENDORS memory.py and rating.py verbatim (importing them from the parent would execute tradingagents.agents.__init__ and its eager LangChain imports â€” verified in this judging pass). File format preserved byte-for-byte so the log at ~/.tradingagents/memory/trading_memory.md (env TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH) stays interoperable with the parent: tag lines '[{date} | {ticker} | {rating} | pending]' -> resolved '[{date} | {ticker} | {rating} | {raw:+.1%} | {alpha:+.1%} | {days}d]', DECISION:/REFLECTION: sections, '<!-- ENTRY_END -->' separators, atomic temp-file + os.replace rewrites, optional memory_log_max_entries rotation (pending entries never pruned). Two-phase protocol unchanged: Phase A at run end â€” store_decision appends the pending entry (no LLM, idempotent tag-line scan). Phase B at the start of the next same-ticker run â€” yfinance 5-day raw return + alpha vs SPY (keyless), then ONE quick-tier query() per resolved entry using the verbatim Reflector prompt (exactly 2-4 sentences plain prose), batch-written atomically; unresolvable entries stay pending and self-heal on later runs. Context injection unchanged: get_past_context(ticker, n_same=5, n_cross=3) computed once per run into state['past_context'], consumed by exactly one prompt â€” the Portfolio Manager â€” guarded by the only-when-non-empty lessons line (issue-#572) so an empty log can never fabricate 'past lessons'. The Reflector call rides the same subscription-auth client as everything else; the entire learn-from-outcomes loop needs no key of any kind. Default routine config points TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH at the shared default; README documents pointing it at a port-specific file if the user ever runs parent and port concurrently (single-process atomic-replace is safe, concurrent writers are not).
final_design.routine_strategy:
AUTH (one-time): run `claude setup-token` interactively on this machine; register_task.ps1 stores the resulting CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN in Windows Credential Manager (primary, avoids plaintext) with an optional -PersistUserEnv fallback that writes it via [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(...,'User') (Candidate 3 graft, for users without the CredentialManager module). The pip claude-agent-sdk auto-detects the env var; no Claude Code login session or browser is needed at run time. The client pops ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the environment at startup, so unattended runs can only draw from the subscription / Agent SDK monthly credit pool (Pro $20 / Max5x $100 / Max20x $200 from 2026-06-15) â€” never metered billing. ToS note in README: the token is licensed for Claude Code/Agent SDK use, personal, non-poolable; this personal daily routine qualifies.

ENTRY POINT: routine.py (console script `tradingagents-cc routine`, also python -m tradingagents_cc.routine): (1) preflight auth (assert token, pop API key; auth failure -> exit 2 with one-line remediation log); (2) acquire single-instance lock file {results_dir}/.routine.lock with PID + stale-lock detection (Candidate 3 graft â€” overlapping runs impossible even if Task Scheduler misfires); (3) cheap weekday guard, then trading-day check (no SPY rows today via yfinance -> log and exit 0); (4) load config/routine.toml (tickers, depth, analysts, model overrides, language); (5) per ticker sequentially: Phase-B resolve -> past_context -> pipeline with per-ticker exception isolation; (6) decisions.jsonl row + report tree per ticker; (7) dated file log under {results_dir}/logs (Task Scheduler swallows stdout); (8) monthly cumulative token-usage tally logged so credit headroom is visible; (9) exit 0 ok/skip, 1 partial, 2 fatal/auth.

SCHEDULING: scripts/register_task.ps1 runs Register-ScheduledTask with: Action = scripts/run_daily.ps1 via powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File (absolute path), WorkingDirectory = repo root (Task Scheduler defaults cwd to system32); Trigger = weekly Mon-Fri at 18:30 local (after US close so same-day OHLCV exists, configurable -Time); Settings = -AllowStartIfOnBatteries -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries -StartWhenAvailable (catch-up after sleep) -MultipleInstances IgnoreNew -ExecutionTimeLimit (New-TimeSpan -Hours 2) -RestartCount 2 -RestartInterval (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 30) (union of Candidates 1+3); 'Run whether user is logged on or not' documented. run_daily.ps1: loads the token from Credential Manager (or relies on User-scope env), sets $env:PYTHONUTF8='1' (Candidate 2 graft â€” console encoding), invokes the workspace venv interpreter DIRECTLY by absolute path (C:\Users\randl\Documents\GitHub\TradingAgents\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m tradingagents_cc.routine â€” sidesteps both the venv-activation pitfall and any uv.exe-location fragility; Candidate 3 graft), tees output to %LOCALAPPDATA%\tradingagents-cc\logs\routine_YYYYMMDD.log, propagates the exit code. All paths absolute.

RESILIENCE: -StartWhenAvailable + always-on checkpointing means a missed/killed run resumes from the last completed node on the task retry or next day; token expiry surfaces as exit 2 with a distinct log line visible in Task Scheduler history/Event Viewer ('re-run claude setup-token'); yfinance failures degrade to error-string reports rather than aborting; pending memory entries retry automatically. Budget: ~13-16 LLM calls/run at sonnet-quick/opus-deep â‰ˆ $0.30-0.80-equivalent against the dedicated Agent SDK monthly credit â€” 1-3 tickers daily at depth 1 fits comfortably on Pro; per-run usage in decisions.jsonl plus the monthly tally make burn observable.
final_design.testing_strategy: Three layers; the default pytest run consumes ZERO subscription usage and zero network. (1) MOCK MODE (built-in, not test-only): mock.py provides MockAgentClient behind the same AgentClient protocol, selected by config llm_backend='mock' or TRADINGAGENTS_CC_MOCK=1; it NEVER imports claude_agent_sdk, is deterministic (seeded by role+ticker), and returns role-appropriate canned outputs â€” analyst markdown reports with tables, debate prose (node code applies the 'Bull Analyst: ' prefixes so the real string-prefix routing is exercised), VALID ResearchPlan/TraderProposal/PortfolioDecision instances, a 2-sentence Reflector blurb â€” plus a fail_structured knob that simulates error_max_structured_output_retries to cover the free-text fallback branch. In mock mode analyst nodes skip the MCP server and Phase B is skipped, so no yfinance traffic either. (2) TEST SUITE (pytest, workspace-registered): conftest.py forces the mock backend, DELETES both CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the test environment so any accidental real-SDK path fails loudly (Candidate 3 graft), redirects TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR/CACHE_DIR/MEMORY_LOG_PATH to tmp_path, and installs a socket-blocking fixture. Coverage: full mock pipeline run asserts the 7 report sections, final decision in the 5-tier vocab, debate counts exactly 2N and risk counts exactly 3N for depths 1/3/5, Bull first/Bear last, Aggressive->Conservative->Neutral order, pending memory entry appended, decisions.jsonl row schema, checkpoint resume skips completed nodes and clears on success; golden tests for render_* shapes ('FINAL TRANSACTION PROPOSAL: **BUY**', '**Rating**: X') and parse_rating round-trips including free-text fallback inputs; vendored TradingMemoryLog lifecycle tests (pending->resolved, ENTRY_END, rotation, idempotent store); data-tool layer with route_to_vendor monkeypatched to recorded yfinance string fixtures (passthrough, comma-split indicators, is_error-on-exception, allowed_tools lists never contain built-ins); Phase-B reflection with stubbed fetch_returns; a parent-seam import canary asserting route_to_vendor/set_config/safe_ticker_component still import framework-free and match expected signatures (Candidate 3 graft, guards against parent refactors). (3) DRY-RUN + OPT-IN LIVE: `tradingagents-cc run --ticker NVDA --dry-run` = mock backend + full artifact writing, a free end-to-end plumbing check on the scheduler machine; `register_task.ps1 -DryRun` registers a task that runs routine --dry-run once to validate the full Task Scheduler wiring for free (Candidate 3 graft). A pytest 'live' marker gated on TRADINGAGENTS_CC_LIVE=1 performs one no-tool haiku query ('reply OK') to verify subscription auth end-to-end for a few hundred tokens; the documented cheapest realism check is a manual `run --ticker SPY --depth 1 --quick-model claude-haiku-4-5`. Burn-prevention invariant: the SDK client class is not even constructed unless backend='sdk', so CI and default pytest cannot spend credit.

file_plan.purpose: Package metadata, workspace dependency wiring, console script, pytest config (rewrite of the bare scaffold)
file_plan.key_contents: name tradingagents-cc; requires-python >=3.10; dependencies = ['tradingagents', 'claude-agent-sdk' pinned to the version resolved in Wave 0, 'pydantic>=2', 'yfinance']; [tool.uv.sources] tradingagents = { workspace = true }; [project.scripts] tradingagents-cc = 'tradingagents_cc.cli:main'; [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths=['tests'], markers = ['live: requires TRADINGAGENTS_CC_LIVE=1 and a real token']

file_plan.purpose: Re-pin the scaffold interpreter from 3.14 to the workspace's 3.13 (verified mismatch)
file_plan.key_contents: single line: 3.13

file_plan.purpose: Package marker (flat package layout â€” the only __init__)
file_plan.key_contents: __version__ = '0.1.0'; no side-effect imports

file_plan.purpose: Single config dict + loader mirroring the parent's surviving keys
file_plan.key_contents: quick_think_llm='claude-sonnet-4-6', deep_think_llm='claude-opus-4-8' (aliases sonnet/opus/haiku accepted), llm_backend='sdk'|'mock' (TRADINGAGENTS_CC_MOCK=1 override), max_debate_rounds=1, max_risk_discuss_rounds=1, max_analyst_turns=12, selected_analysts, output_language='English', checkpoint_enabled=True (always-on default, disablable), results_dir/data_cache_dir/memory_log_path honoring TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR/CACHE_DIR/MEMORY_LOG_PATH with ~/.tradingagents defaults, memory_log_max_entries=None, data_vendors all 'yfinance' + tool_vendors {} passthrough, bind_insider_to_news=False, anthropic_effort=None, retry settings; to_parent_config() helper feeding tradingagents.dataflows.config.set_config

file_plan.purpose: The one LLM seam: SDK client with subscription-auth guard, retry, stats, structured handling
file_plan.key_contents: AgentResult dataclass {text, structured, usage{llm_calls,tool_calls,tokens_in,tokens_out}, num_turns, tool_call_log}; AgentClient protocol; AuthError/StageError/StructuredOutputFailed exceptions; SdkAgentClient: pops ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (logged warning) + asserts CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, builds ClaudeAgentOptions (model, system_prompt, mcp_servers, allowed_tools, disallowed_tools covering all built-ins, can_use_tool deny-by-default, max_turns, setting_sources=[], permission_mode='acceptEdits', output_format json_schema when schema given, effort passthrough), iterates query() messages counting AssistantMessages/tool_use blocks + summing ResultMessage usage + collecting tool-call log lines for message_tool.log, maps subtype error_max_structured_output_retries to structured=None for caller fallback, retry wrapper 3x backoff 5s/15s/45s + jitter; get_client(config) factory (only constructs SDK client when backend='sdk'); module docstring: Windows Proactor event-loop requirement

file_plan.purpose: Deterministic mock backend â€” zero subscription usage, zero network, never imports claude_agent_sdk
file_plan.key_contents: MockAgentClient implementing AgentClient: canned per-role outputs seeded by (role, ticker) â€” analyst markdown reports with tables, debate prose, valid ResearchPlan/TraderProposal/PortfolioDecision instances, 2-sentence reflector blurb; fail_structured knob returning structured=None to exercise the free-text fallback; raise_at_stage knob for checkpoint-resume tests; zero-usage AgentResults

file_plan.purpose: In-process MCP server exposing the 9 keyless data tools with original names/signatures
file_plan.key_contents: Nine @tool handlers (get_stock_data, get_indicators with comma-split + per-indicator ValueError collection, get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement, get_news, get_global_news, get_insider_transactions) each awaiting asyncio.to_thread(route_to_vendor, ...) with try/except -> {'is_error': True} text result; ToolAnnotations(readOnlyHint=True); create_sdk_mcp_server(name='data'); ANALYST_TOOLSETS = {market: [stock_data, indicators], social: [news], news: [news, global_news] (+insider_transactions when bind_insider_to_news), fundamentals: [4 statement tools]} with fully-qualified mcp__data__ names; apply_data_config(cfg) calling tradingagents.dataflows.config.set_config

file_plan.purpose: All prompt text, ported verbatim from the parent agents (prompts are inline in LangChain closures and cannot be imported) â€” each constant cites its source file
file_plan.key_contents: Collaboration wrapper ('You are a helpful AI assistant, collaborating... FINAL TRANSACTION PROPOSAL...'); 4 analyst system_messages (market indicator catalog, social, news, fundamentals with the 1-tuple bug FIXED to a plain string); bull/bear researcher prompt builders; Research Manager prompt with 5-tier Rating Scale block; trader system+user; 3 risk-debator prompts; Portfolio Manager prompt with conditional lessons_line; REFLECTOR_SYSTEM + reflector human template; build_instrument_context(); get_language_instruction() (analysts + PM only)

file_plan.purpose: Vendored Pydantic schemas + canonical markdown renderers (cannot import from parent: tradingagents.agents.__init__ eagerly pulls LangChain â€” verified)
file_plan.key_contents: PortfolioRating 5-tier enum, TraderAction 3-tier enum; ResearchPlan/TraderProposal/PortfolioDecision (shallow schemas, optional fields not required); render_research_plan ('**Recommendation**: X...'), render_trader_proposal (always appends 'FINAL TRANSACTION PROPOSAL: **X**'), render_pm_decision (always emits '**Rating**: X'); model_json_schema() consumed by client output_format

file_plan.purpose: Vendored deterministic signal extraction (byte-for-byte from parent agents/utils/rating.py)
file_plan.key_contents: RATINGS_5_TIER tuple; parse_rating(text, default='Hold'): pass-1 per-line regex rating.*?[:\-][\s*]*(\w+), pass-2 first bare 5-tier word stripped of *:.,, Title-cased output; no LLM

file_plan.purpose: Vendored TradingMemoryLog (byte-identical file format, interoperable with the parent's log)
file_plan.key_contents: store_decision (pending tag, idempotent line scan), get_past_context(n_same=5, n_cross=3), batch_update_with_outcomes (atomic temp + os.replace), rotation with pending-never-pruned, '<!-- ENTRY_END -->' separators; pure stdlib + local parse_rating import

file_plan.purpose: State contract
file_plan.key_contents: AgentState/InvestDebateState/RiskDebateState TypedDicts (original fields minus the messages channel); create_initial_state(company_name, trade_date, past_context='') zeroing both debate states exactly; ticker validation re-exporting safe_ticker_component from tradingagents.dataflows.utils; normalize_ticker = strip().upper()

file_plan.purpose: Routing predicates ported verbatim (trust the formulas, not the stale '3 rounds' comments)
file_plan.key_contents: should_continue_debate(state, max_debate_rounds): count >= 2*N -> done, else 'Bear' if current_response.startswith('Bull') else 'Bull'; should_continue_risk_analysis(state, max_risk_discuss_rounds): count >= 3*N -> done, else Aggressive->Conservative->Neutral keyed on latest_speaker startswith prefixes

file_plan.purpose: One async function per graph node, taking/returning state-dict updates exactly like the LangGraph nodes
file_plan.key_contents: run_analyst(state, kind, client, cfg) -> {<x>_report} via one tool-enabled query (ANALYST_TOOLSETS scoping); run_bull/run_bear (verbatim prompts, 'Bull Analyst: ' prefixes, history/count updates); run_research_manager/run_trader/run_portfolio_manager implementing structured-attempt-then-one-shot-free-text fallback (invoke_structured_or_freetext parity) + render_*; run_risk_debator(role) setting latest_speaker exactly 'Aggressive'/'Conservative'/'Neutral'; reflect_on_decision() one-shot quick call

file_plan.purpose: The orchestrator replacing TradingAgentsGraph + GraphSetup + Propagator
file_plan.key_contents: class TradingAgentsPipeline(config, client): async propagate(ticker, trade_date) -> (final_state, decision); sequence: Phase-B resolve -> get_past_context -> create_initial_state -> sequential analysts -> Bull-first debate loop -> RM -> Trader -> Aggressive-first risk loop -> PM -> parse_rating -> reporting tree + full_states_log JSON + decisions.jsonl -> store_decision -> clear checkpoint; checkpoint save after each node + resume skip via completed-node list; per-node stage timings + aggregated stats {llm_calls, tool_calls, tokens_in, tokens_out}; fallbacks_used flags

file_plan.purpose: SqliteSaver replacement: always-on stage-level JSON resume
file_plan.key_contents: thread_id(ticker, date) = sha256(f'{TICKER}:{date}').hexdigest()[:16] (parent-identical); atomic temp + os.replace write of {thread_id, completed: [exact original node-name strings], state} to {data_cache_dir}/cc_checkpoints/{safe_ticker}/{thread_id}.json; load/save/clear/clear_all; ticker validated via safe_ticker_component

file_plan.purpose: Phase-B outcome resolution (parent _resolve_pending_entries/_fetch_returns ported off LangChain, ~40 lines)
file_plan.key_contents: fetch_returns(ticker, trade_date, holding_days=5): yfinance ticker+SPY history over date..+12d, actual_days=min(5, len-1), alpha = raw - spy_return, (None,None,None) on insufficient data (entry stays pending); resolve_pending_entries(memory_log, ticker, client): one quick-tier max_turns=1 no-tool reflector call per resolved entry then batch_update_with_outcomes; all failures logged + non-fatal; skipped in mock mode

file_plan.purpose: Per-run markdown reports + machine-readable decision log + message_tool.log (hard requirement 6)
file_plan.key_contents: Live tee {results_dir}/{TICKER}/{date}/reports/{section}.md for the 7 canonical sections with original '### Bull Researcher Analysis'/'### Research Manager Decision'/'### {X} Analyst Analysis' headers; message_tool.log writer ('HH:MM:SS [Tool Call] name(args)' from AgentResult.tool_call_log â€” Candidate 2 graft); complete_report.md with '## I. Analyst Team Reports'..'## V. Portfolio Manager Decision'; full_states_log_{date}.json under {results_dir}/{TICKER}/TradingAgentsStrategy_logs/ (same keys as parent _log_state); append_decision(decisions.jsonl): {schema_version, run_id, ts, ticker, trade_date, decision, rating, trader_action, status, error, models, stats, stage_timings, fallbacks_used, report_dir, duration_s}; safe_ticker_component on every path join

file_plan.purpose: argparse CLI entry (no Rich/questionary)
file_plan.key_contents: subcommands: run TICKER [--date YYYY-MM-DD not-future, default today] [--depth 1|3|5 -> sets BOTH round knobs] [--analysts m,s,n,f] [--language] [--quick-model/--deep-model] [--mock] [--dry-run] [--no-checkpoint]; resume TICKER --date; routine [--dry-run]; clear-checkpoints; show-decisions [-n 10] (tail of decisions.jsonl, Candidate 3 graft); ticker normalized strip().upper() (restores normalization the parent CLI dropped) + safe_ticker_component check; main() entry for console script

file_plan.purpose: Unattended daily driver for Task Scheduler
file_plan.key_contents: main(): file logging to dated log under {results_dir}/logs; preflight auth (exit 2 + remediation line on failure); single-instance lock file {results_dir}/.routine.lock with PID + stale-lock detection; weekday guard then SPY-rows trading-day check (exit 0 skip); load config/routine.toml; per-ticker sequential propagate with per-ticker exception isolation; monthly token-usage tally; aggregate exit codes 0/1/2; if __name__ == '__main__' so python -m tradingagents_cc.routine works

file_plan.purpose: Declarative routine config
file_plan.key_contents: tickers = ['SPY']; depth = 1; analysts = ['market','social','news','fundamentals']; quick_model/deep_model overrides; output_language = 'English'; checkpoint_enabled = true; comments documenting every key

file_plan.purpose: Task Scheduler wrapper
file_plan.key_contents: Reads CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN from Windows Credential Manager into $env: (falls through to User-scope env var if already set); $env:PYTHONUTF8='1'; invokes C:\Users\randl\Documents\GitHub\TradingAgents\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m tradingagents_cc.routine by absolute path (no venv activation, no uv dependency at run time); tees output to %LOCALAPPDATA%\tradingagents-cc\logs\routine_YYYYMMDD.log; propagates exit code

file_plan.purpose: One-shot scheduler registration
file_plan.key_contents: Params -Time (default 18:30), -TaskName TradingAgentsCC, -DryRun (registers a task running routine --dry-run once), -PersistUserEnv; prompts for token and stores in Credential Manager (or [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable User scope); Register-ScheduledTask: Mon-Fri trigger, Action powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File run_daily.ps1 with WorkingDirectory = repo root, Settings -AllowStartIfOnBatteries -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries -StartWhenAvailable -MultipleInstances IgnoreNew -ExecutionTimeLimit 2h -RestartCount 2 -RestartInterval 30min; prints verification commands (Start-ScheduledTask + log path); 'run whether user is logged on or not' guidance

file_plan.purpose: Test isolation guaranteeing zero subscription/network usage
file_plan.key_contents: Autouse fixtures: force llm_backend='mock'; monkeypatch.delenv CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (accidental real-SDK path fails loudly); redirect TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR/CACHE_DIR/MEMORY_LOG_PATH to tmp_path; socket-blocking fixture; route_to_vendor fixture loader over tests/fixtures/ recorded yfinance strings; 'live' marker auto-skip unless TRADINGAGENTS_CC_LIVE=1

file_plan.purpose: End-to-end mock pipeline + resume assertions
file_plan.key_contents: Full run asserts: 7 sections written; decision in 5-tier vocab; debate count == 2*N and risk count == 3*N for depths 1/3/5; Bull first/Bear last; Aggressive->Conservative->Neutral order; pending memory entry appended; decisions.jsonl row schema + fallbacks_used flag when fail_structured set; checkpoint resume test (mock raise_at_stage -> rerun skips completed nodes, identical final state, cleared on success); past_context lessons line present only when log non-empty

file_plan.purpose: Unit tests for vendored + ported pieces and parent-seam canary
file_plan.key_contents: parse_rating golden cases incl. free-text fallback inputs; render_* golden markdown ('FINAL TRANSACTION PROPOSAL: **BUY**', '**Rating**: X'); TradingMemoryLog lifecycle (pending->resolved tag rewrite, ENTRY_END, rotation, idempotent store); conditional_logic formulas at N=1,3,5; tools layer with monkeypatched route_to_vendor (string passthrough, comma-split indicators, is_error path, ANALYST_TOOLSETS contain no built-in tool names); reflection with stubbed fetch_returns; parent-seam import canary (route_to_vendor/set_config/safe_ticker_component import without LangChain and match signatures); options-builder test asserting disallowed_tools always covers Bash/WebSearch/Read/Write/Task

file_plan.purpose: Setup and operations runbook (replaces empty scaffold README)
file_plan.key_contents: Prereqs (uv sync at workspace root, Python 3.13); claude setup-token -> register_task.ps1 flow; ToS + monthly Agent SDK credit-pool notes (Pro $20 / Max5x $100 / Max20x $200 from 2026-06-15); explicit statement that zero API keys are required, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is removed from the environment by design, and Alpha Vantage is opt-in; config reference; mock/dry-run vs cheap live smoke instructions; resumability + troubleshooting (token expiry exit 2, yfinance 429s, log locations, Proactor-loop note, moved-repo caveat); decisions.jsonl schema; memory-log interop note

file_plan.purpose: Replace the scaffold hello-world with the minimal programmatic example (parity with parent main.py â€” Candidate 3 graft)
file_plan.key_contents: Builds config, runs one pipeline for ('NVDA', yesterday) in mock mode by default (--live opt-in), prints the 5-tier signal and report path via asyncio.run

risks: Subscription/ToS and quota: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is licensed only for Claude Code / Agent SDK use (this project qualifies; personal, non-poolable); from 2026-06-15 headless runs draw from the dedicated monthly Agent SDK credit pool (Pro $20 / Max5x $100 / Max20x $200) â€” a long ticker list or depth 5 on Opus could exhaust Pro mid-month, pausing runs until reset. Mitigated by per-run usage in decisions.jsonl, the routine's monthly tally, depth-1 default, and the haiku quick-tier override.

OAuth token expiry/revocation with no SDK auto-refresh: unattended runs fail until the user re-runs `claude setup-token`. Mitigated by fail-fast preflight (exit 2, distinct log line, visible in Task Scheduler history/Event Viewer), but there is no push alerting by default â€” a notify-on-failure stub (flag file) is the documented extension point.

claude-agent-sdk API drift: output_format/structured_output shapes, ResultMessage subtypes, and ClaudeAgentOptions fields are evolving surfaces; the research snapshots are mid-2026. Mitigated by verifying the installed SDK surface in Wave 0 before writing client.py, pinning the version in pyproject, and gating upgrades on the cheap live smoke test (mock tests cannot catch wire-level changes).

yfinance fragility (the only data source by default): unofficial Yahoo endpoints can 429 (yf_retry covers transient cases), change schemas, or return sparse/empty news; get_fundamentals is a current-snapshot look-ahead leak for historical dates (inherited upstream quirk, documented not fixed). Failures degrade to error-string reports and pending memory entries rather than aborting; a Yahoo breaking change requires a parent-package update, surfaced by the canary test and per-run logs.

Parent-package coupling: reusing tradingagents.dataflows via the uv workspace means parent refactors of route_to_vendor/set_config/safe_ticker_component can break the port. Mitigated by the deliberately thin three-symbol import surface, the parent-seam canary test, the workspace lockfile pinning one resolved version, and vendoring as the documented fallback. The parent's LangChain deps land in the shared venv but are never imported by tradingagents-cc at runtime.

Structured-output retry exhaustion (subtype error_max_structured_output_retries): the one-shot free-text fallback plus parse_rating(default='Hold') keeps the pipeline alive, but a persistently malformed PM output silently biases decisions toward Hold. Mitigated by fallback prompts that explicitly demand the '**Rating**: X' header and by flagging fallbacks_used per run in decisions.jsonl so drift is observable.

Built-in tool leakage: misconfigured allowed/disallowed lists could let analyst agents run Bash/WebSearch/Read on the host. Double-enforced (allowlist + can_use_tool deny-by-default) and covered by a unit test asserting constructed options never include built-ins and ANALYST_TOOLSETS contain only mcp__data__ names.

Windows unattended quirks: Task Scheduler cwd defaults to system32, swallows stdout, does not activate venvs, and PowerShell execution policy can block scripts; the SDK's subprocess transport needs the Proactor event loop and console encoding wants PYTHONUTF8=1. All routed around by design (absolute paths, direct venv python.exe, file logging, -ExecutionPolicy Bypass, PYTHONUTF8 in the wrapper), but a moved repo or recreated .venv silently breaks the task until the log/Event Viewer is checked â€” documented in README troubleshooting.

Single shared memory log: atomic temp+os.replace writes are safe for one process but not for the parent project and the port writing the same file at the same instant. Mitigated by the routine's single-instance lock, sequential ticker processing, and a documented option to point TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH at a port-specific file.

Prompt snapshot drift: prompts.py (and vendored memory/rating/schemas) are verbatim copies because the parent embeds prompts in LangChain closures and gates the small modules behind a LangChain-importing package __init__; upstream improvements will not flow automatically. Each constant cites its source file so a periodic manual diff is cheap, but it is a standing maintenance obligation.

Mock-mode divergence: the mock backend validates plumbing, formats, routing, and resume â€” not model behavior (schema compliance under pressure, tool-loop length, prompt regressions). Mitigated by max_turns caps, live section-md tees preserving partial output from failed runs, and the documented occasional live smoke (haiku, depth 1, one ticker).

Behavioral fidelity is structural, not textual: Claude will produce different reports/debates than the parent's gpt-5.4-tier defaults, and the Claude Code runtime injects its own scaffolding around system prompts; setting_sources=[] and strict tool lists minimize variance, but exact output parity with the parent is impossible by definition â€” the contract preserved is pipeline shape, prompts, markers, and artifacts.

implementation_order: WAVE 0 â€” repo hygiene + skeleton (sequential, one implementer): delete the nested C:\Users\randl\Documents\GitHub\TradingAgents\tradingagents-cc\.git directory; write .python-version (3.13), pyproject.toml, and tradingagents_cc\__init__.py; run `uv sync` at the workspace root to resolve and pin the claude-agent-sdk version; inspect the installed SDK (ClaudeAgentOptions fields, output_format/structured_output, ResultMessage subtypes) and record the confirmed surface for Wave 2 implementers; agree the AgentClient/AgentResult and pipeline.propagate signatures as the frozen interfaces.

WAVE 1 â€” leaf modules (fully parallel, no shared files, no cross-imports beyond the package marker): default_config.py | prompts.py | schemas.py | rating.py | memory.py | state.py | conditional_logic.py | config\routine.toml.

WAVE 2 â€” seam + infrastructure modules (fully parallel; depend only on Wave 1 modules and the frozen AgentClient interface): client.py | mock.py | tools_data.py | checkpointer.py | reflection.py | reporting.py.
WAVE 3 â€” nodes.py (single implementer; imports prompts, schemas, conditional_logic, client interface, tools_data).
WAVE 4 â€” pipeline.py (single implementer; composes nodes, checkpointer, reflection, reporting, memory, rating).

WAVE 5 â€” entry points, ops artifacts, docs (fully parallel; all consume the frozen pipeline.propagate signature, no shared files): cli.py | routine.py | scripts\run_daily.ps1 | scripts\register_task.ps1 | main.py | README.md.

WAVE 6 â€” tests (fully parallel, disjoint files): tests\conftest.py | tests\test_pipeline_mock.py | tests\test_units.py (plus tests\fixtures\ recorded yfinance strings, owned by the conftest implementer).

WAVE 7 â€” verification (sequential): run `uv run --project tradingagents-cc pytest` (mock-only, zero network/credit â€” socket guard and deleted auth env vars enforce this); run `tradingagents-cc run SPY --dry-run` to validate the full artifact tree; run `scripts\register_task.ps1 -DryRun` to validate scheduler wiring; optionally (user-initiated, ~hundreds of tokens) TRADINGAGENTS_CC_LIVE=1 live auth smoke, then one manual `run SPY --depth 1 --quick-model claude-haiku-4-5` before enabling the real schedule.
